docs.title: Information Flow Enforcement Policy
docs.flow_control_policy: Traffic may flow only along explicitly approved paths: internet -> public ALB (443 only) -> app tier -> data tier; the data tier accepts connections solely from the app tier security group and denies all egress to the internet. Deny-by-default is the baseline for every zone boundary.
docs.boundary_definitions: Three security zones inside the authorization boundary: public DMZ (10.0.0.0/24), private application tier (10.0.10.0/24), and restricted data tier (10.0.20.0/24), each mapped to a dedicated VPC subnet and moderate-impact data-classification tier.
docs.enforcement_mechanisms: AWS security groups and network ACLs enforce zone-to-zone flows, an egress proxy mediates outbound HTTP(S), and a web application firewall inspects north-south traffic; VPC flow logs are shipped to the SIEM for reconciliation against this approved-flow matrix.
